authors: Alex Osterwalder, Yves Pigneur, Greg Bernarda, Alan Smith

chapters.title: Canvas
chapters.summary: Introduces the Value Proposition Canvas with its two sides—Customer Profile and Value Map—and explains how to achieve Fit between them.

key_images.caption: The Fit concept diagram showing the relationship between creating value and observing customers
A filled-in Value Proposition Canvas example mapping the Value Proposition Design book itself
Step-by-step exercise for mapping how products and services create value

chapters.title: Design
chapters.summary: Covers the messy, nonlinear process of designing value propositions through prototyping, understanding customers, making choices, and finding the right business model.

key_images.caption: The design squiggle illustrating the nonlinear journey from chaos to clarity in inventing and improving value propositions
Overview of five prototyping techniques: napkin sketches, ad-libs, Value Proposition Canvases, representations, and MVPs
The Journalist interview process: a 7-step loop for conducting customer interviews to gain better understanding

chapters.title: Test
chapters.summary: Describes how to reduce risk by extracting hypotheses, designing experiments with the Test Card, and systematically validating or invalidating assumptions.

key_images.caption: Illustrated testing factory showing the flow from design through hypothesis extraction, experimentation, data analysis, and learning

chapters.title: Evolve
chapters.summary: Explains how to continuously evolve value propositions by creating alignment, measuring progress, improving relentlessly, and reinventing constantly.
